text.title: rust-testing-unit Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for rust-testing-unit as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Two #[tokio::test]s sharing a lazy_static mock state — passing locally, failing under cargo test -- --test-threads=N.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Tests run in parallel by default. Shared mutable global state (lazy_static, once_cell) produces order-dependent failures. Force per-test setup via a fixture struct or local variable.

failure-mode.description: Mixing #[tokio::test] with #[test] on async blocks compiled under different runtime flavors (current_thread vs multi_thread) in the same test binary.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Flakes under contention because the runtime scheduler interleaves tasks across the two flavors unpredictably in the same process.

failure-mode.description: Defining a one-method trait just to get mockall to mock a free function.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A function-pointer field or generic parameter is cleaner. Trait gymnastics multiply compile time and produce confusing mock! macro error messages.
